Tests the behavior of `Math.floor` with positive zero in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's `Math.floor` function when applied to the positive zero value (`+0`). The expression `1/Math.floor(+0)` is expected to evaluate to `Infinity`, which is consistent with the mathematical operation of dividing by zero. The use of `assert` ensures that the program will throw an error if the condition is not met, indicating a failure in the expected behavior of the `Math.floor` function. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case in mathematical operations within JavaScript.
